meta.title: G21 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 3 Series G21 engine — B48, B58, B47, B57, 330e PHEV. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and a clear 70% BMW threshold. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series G21 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G21 engine typically costs £2,500–£8,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on engine — the B48 four-cylinder sits at £2,800–£5,200, the B58 straight-six at £4,500–£8,000, and the 330e PHEV at £4,000–£7,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,170. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 3 Series G21 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol in 320i form (184 bhp) is the most reliable G21 engine — it's proven across BMW's CLAR platform, avoids the complexity of the 330e's hybrid system or the diesel's DPF considerations, and offers strong performance for a sensible price. The B58 straight-six is also highly robust.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series G21?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common issues include: cooling system failures (water pump, thermostat) on the B48 and B58 petrol engines; timing chain wear on higher-mileage B47 diesels; DPF clogging on diesel variants used primarily for short urban trips; and battery degradation on the 330e plug-in hybrid.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 3 Series G21 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Overall, yes — the G21's B48 and B58 petrol engines are modern and highly reliable. The B47 diesel is significantly more robust than its N47 predecessor. The 330e PHEV is more complex but well-supported. The M340i's B58 is reliable but places additional strain on the cooling system.

mainEntity.name: Which G21 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: We don't recommend outright avoidance — but a B47 diesel without regular service history, particularly if used for short urban trips where DPF clogging is more likely, is one to approach with caution. For the 330e, ensure the battery health is documented and the cooling system is in good condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G21 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs: B48/B58 — coolant loss with no external leak, overheating, or water pump noise; B47 — timing chain rattle on cold start, DPF warning light, or loss of power; 330e — reduced electric range, battery warning lights, or cooling system faults. Use our diagnostic guide to identify the specific symptoms.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 3 Series G21 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply our 70% BMW threshold. A £4,800 B48 recon against a £25,000 G21 is just 19% — well within the threshold. Even the M340i's £8,000 recon against a £35,000 car is just 23%. Unlike older BMWs, the G21's high retained value means engine replacement is almost always financially viable. Value the car first — but on a modern G21, a recon is usually a repair decision, not a financial one.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 3 Series G21 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 and B58 petrols can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The B47 diesel is robust and can reach 150,000+ miles with regular maintenance. The 330e's battery health is the limiting factor — expect 8–10 years of service before significant degradation.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G20, G21, G80 and G81?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G20 is the seventh-generation 3 Series saloon (2019–present). The G21 is the Touring (estate) variant (2019–present). The G80 is the M3 saloon (2020–present). The G81 is the M3 Touring (2023–present). All share the CLAR platform but have different engine lineups — the G21 Touring shares its engines with the G20 saloon.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 3 Series G21 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping varies by engine. For the B48, the code is on the block near the exhaust manifold. For the B58 and B57, it's on the block face below the inlet manifold. Tell us your registration and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: BMW 3 Series G21 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G21 Touring — from the 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrols and diesels to the 3.0-litre straight-six M340i and the 330e plug-in hybrid. Real UK replacement costs, honest failure data, and the coolant system and timing chain issues that define this generation's ownership story. Backed by real UK enquiry data [PATTERN DATA].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 3 Series G21 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G21 Touring — from the 2019 launch to the present, including the 2022 LCI updates and the 2024 diesel phase-out.

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are provisional — no dedicated Engine Page exists for any G21 engine family. The B48 and B58 are considered highly reliable, with the B58 particularly robust. The B47 diesel is significantly more reliable than its N47 predecessor but still has known timing chain considerations. The 330e PHEV adds complexity with its hybrid system.

overview.intro: The G21 is the Touring (estate) variant of the seventh-generation BMW 3 Series, launched in 2019 as the successor to the F31 Touring. It shares the CLAR platform with the G20 saloon and offers the same range of four-cylinder and six-cylinder petrol and diesel engines, plus the 330e plug-in hybrid. A 2022 facelift (LCI) brought updated styling and technology, while 2024 saw the removal of diesel options from the UK line-up. The range-topping M340i xDrive produces 374 bhp from its B58 straight-six, and the M3 Touring (G81) sits above as the performance flagship.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR (Cluster Architecture) • Predecessor: F31 • Successor: TBA • Sibling body styles in this generation: G20 (saloon), G80 (M3 saloon), G81 (M3 Touring).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The G21 Touring remains a popular choice in the UK premium estate segment, with strong demand for the 320i petrol and 330e plug-in hybrid variants [PATTERN DATA].

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode — exactly one paragraph: The B58 3.0-litre straight-six in the M340i xDrive is the jewel of the G21 range — 374 bhp, smooth, sonorous, and one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. The B48 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol in 320i form (184 bhp) is the sensible choice: proven, efficient, and free from the complexity of the 330e's hybrid system or the diesel's DPF considerations. The 330e plug-in hybrid offers excellent company-car tax benefits and strong performance (292 bhp), but its hybrid system adds complexity and potential battery costs. The B47 diesels are robust and economical, but their UK availability ended in 2024. For most buyers, the 320i petrol strikes the best balance of performance, reliability, and running costs.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Petrol — proven, reliable, and the sensible choice.
Strong Choice — more power, same reliable architecture.
Performance Choice — reliable but expensive to replace.
High-Mileage Choice — robust but discontinued in UK.
Diesel Six — torquey and refined, but discontinued.

economicsRule.title: The G21 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,800 B48 recon against a £25,000 G21 sits at 19% — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Even the M340i's £8,000 recon against a £35,000 car is just 23%. Unlike older BMWs, the G21's high retained value means engine replacement is almost always financially viable. Value the car first — but on a modern G21, a recon is usually a repair decision, not a financial one.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G21 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 320i — proven reliability, reasonable running costs, and no hybrid complexity.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G21 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M340i xDrive — higher fuel consumption, more complex drivetrain, and higher replacement costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G21 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 320i — the sweet spot of performance, reliability, and running costs.

problems.title: B48 Cooling System Failure
problems.rootCause: The B48 four-cylinder petrol engine suffers from water pump failure and cooling system leaks. The plastic coolant pipes and thermostat housing can become brittle with age and heat cycling, leading to coolant loss and potential overheating.

tieredCosts.work: Water pump replacement; coolant system inspection

problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £400–£900 water pump replacement is far cheaper than the £2,800–£4,800 engine replacement that follows an overheating failure.

cta.label: Compare B48 engine prices for your G21 →

problems.title: B58 Cooling System and Oil Leak Issues
problems.rootCause: The B58 straight-six is generally robust, but oil leaks from the oil filter housing and valve cover gasket can occur. The cooling system is complex and can develop leaks at the coolant expansion tank and hoses.

tieredCosts.work: Oil leak repair; cooling system inspection

problems.recommendation: On any M340i approaching 60,000 miles, have the cooling system and oil filter housing inspected — a £500–£1,200 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £4,500–£8,000 replacement that follows a major failure.

cta.label: Compare B58 engine prices for your G21 →

problems.title: B47 Diesel Timing Chain and DPF Issues
problems.rootCause: The B47 diesel is significantly more robust than its N47 predecessor, but timing chain wear can still occur on higher-mileage cars with neglected servicing. DPF clogging from short urban trips is also a common issue.
problems.recommendation: On any B47 diesel approaching 60,000 miles, have the timing chain inspected and ensure the DPF is regularly regenerated with motorway driving. A £600–£1,500 preventative service is far cheaper than the £2,800–£4,800 replacement that follows a chain failure.

cta.label: Compare B47 engine prices for your G21 →

problems.title: 330e PHEV Battery and Cooling System Issues
problems.rootCause: The 330e's plug-in hybrid system adds complexity — battery degradation over time, cooling system leaks, and charging system faults can occur. The battery's 19.5 kWh capacity provides up to 63 miles of electric range, but battery health is critical to the car's value.

tieredCosts.work: Battery health check; cooling system service

problems.recommendation: On any 330e approaching 40,000 miles, have the battery health checked and the cooling system inspected — a £600–£1,500 preventative service is far cheaper than the £6,000–£9,000 battery replacement that follows a major failure.

cta.label: Compare 330e battery and engine prices for your G21 →

replacementCosts.h2: G21 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance note required — no Engine Page baseline exists for these engines yet. Costs are sourced from UK specialist suppliers [BMW-QUOTE].

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are BMW 3 Series Touring G21 only. The G20 saloon, G80 M3 saloon and G81 M3 Touring are separate models with different engine availability and are covered on their own pages. Diesel variants were removed from the UK line-up in 2024.
